text.title: Deploy Scripts: Lifecycle and Patterns
text.summary: Deploy lifecycle: pre-checks, rsync patterns, virtualenv management, service restart, validation, and hook system for workspace/runtime separation.

rule.statement: A deploy has five ordered phases: pre-checks → rsync → deps → restart → validate. Never skip pre-checks or post-validate in production.
rule.rationale: Skipping pre-checks lets deploys proceed with a missing env file or full disk, which leaves the runtime in a partial state. Skipping post-validation means a failed restart is not caught until a user hits an error.

example.code: 1. pre-deploy checks — .env exists, disk space, systemd session alive 2. rsync — workspace/repos/<svc>/ → /srv/nero/<svc>/src/ 3. dependencies — pip install -e . (or -r requirements.txt) if changed 4. restart — systemctl --user restart <svc>.service 5. post-validate — wait for active, curl /health if HTTP service

rule.statement: Validate env file, disk space (>1 GB free), systemd user session, and runtime directory existence before any rsync. Exit non-zero on failure.
rule.rationale: A deploy that starts without the env file will sync code correctly but the service will fail to start with a config error. Catching this upfront gives a clear actionable error before any files change.

rule.statement: Use `rsync -a --delete` with excludes for `.git/`, `__pycache__/`, `.venv/`, `.env`, and `node_modules/`. Source path must end with `/` to sync contents, not the directory itself.
rule.rationale: Without the trailing slash, rsync creates a nested directory (`src/nero-core/`) instead of syncing files directly into `src/`. `--delete` ensures removed files are cleaned from the runtime.

antipattern.description: rsync without trailing slash on the source: `rsync -a src $dst/`
antipattern.reason: Syncs the directory itself into the destination, creating `$dst/src/` instead of placing files directly in `$dst/`.

rule.statement: Create the venv once; reinstall deps only when `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml` changes. Use `--rebuild-venv` flag to force recreate after Python version upgrades.
rule.rationale: Running `pip install` on every deploy adds 10-30 seconds to each deploy and generates churn in pip's cache. Diffing the requirements file before installing keeps deploys fast.

example.code:
setup_venv() { local svc_dir="$1" local venv="$svc_dir/.venv"
if [[ "$REBUILD_VENV" == "true" ]] || [[ ! -d "$venv" ]]; then echo " Creating venv..." rm -rf "$venv" python3 -m venv "$venv" fi
local req="$svc_dir/src/requirements.txt" local stamp="$svc_dir/.last-requirements.txt" if [[ -f "$req" ]] && ! diff -q "$req" "$stamp" &>/dev/null; then echo " Installing requirements..." "$venv/bin/pip" install -q -r "$req" cp "$req" "$stamp" fi
# editable install if pyproject.toml present if [[ -f "$svc_dir/src/pyproject.toml" ]]; then "$venv/bin/pip" install -q -e "$svc_dir/src/" fi }

rule.statement: After restarting a systemd service, wait up to 30 seconds for it to become active. For HTTP services, also confirm the health endpoint returns 200.
rule.rationale: A restart command exits successfully even if the process crashes immediately after. Polling `is-active` and then hitting `/health` gives confidence that the new code is actually running and accepting traffic.

section.title: Deploy Hook System
section.p: Hooks let each service extend the deploy lifecycle without modifying the central script. Create shell scripts in `deploy-hooks/` inside the workspace service directory.

rule.statement: Support hooks at: `pre-deploy`, `post-sync`, `pre-restart`, `post-restart`. The central deploy script checks for each file and sources it if present.
rule.rationale: Database migrations (`post-sync`), connection draining (`pre-restart`), and notification webhooks (`post-restart`) are service-specific concerns. Hooks keep the central script generic while allowing per-service customization.

example.code:
run_hook() { local svc="$1" hook="$2" local file="$WORKSPACE/$svc/deploy-hooks/${hook}.sh" [[ -f "$file" ]] && bash "$file" "$svc" "$RUNTIME/$svc" }
# Usage in deploy_systemd(): run_hook "$svc" "post-sync" # run migrations restart_and_check "$svc" "$port" run_hook "$svc" "post-restart" # notify, smoke test

# nero-channel-web/deploy-hooks/post-sync.sh #!/usr/bin/env bash # Run Alembic migrations after code sync /srv/nero/nero-channel-web/.venv/bin/alembic \ --config /srv/nero/nero-channel-web/src/alembic.ini \ upgrade head echo " Migrations complete"

rule.statement: Define a deploy order array: SDK/libraries first, then infra (Docker), then application services, then frontend. When SDK changes, redeploy all dependents.
rule.rationale: A service that restarts before its shared library is updated will start against stale library code. Explicit ordering prevents this class of version mismatch.

example.code:
DEPLOY_ORDER=(nero-sdk nero-infra nero-core nero-channel-web nero-channel-tg nero-web)
# SDK changed: redeploy dependents sdk_dependents=(nero-core nero-channel-web nero-channel-tg)

see-also.link: Full deploy orchestrator with service registry
Deploy output examples, rollback scenario, migration hook
